title: primula auricula subsp. ciliata
intro: Primula auricula subsp. ciliata, also known as ciliated auricula primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the primrose family (Primulaceae) native to the mountains of central and eastern Europe. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Primula auricula subsp. ciliata include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and ease respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for those suffering from these conditions. Herbal preparations of Primula auricula subsp. ciliata include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
